reasoning: Based on extensive research, I estimate a 22% probability of Niger receiving a Level 4 travel advisory before July 1, 2025. Key evidence: (1) Niger was DOWNGRADED from Level 4 to Level 3 on March 21, 2025 - this is strong evidence against imminent escalation; (2) While serious incidents occurred after the downgrade (Fambita mosque attack killing 44+ same day, kidnapping of 5 Indians on April 30), the State Department has not changed the advisory for 6+ weeks, suggesting these don't cross the Level 4 threshold; (3) Level 4 criteria heavily weight wrongful detention of US citizens and direct threats to Americans - no US citizens were kidnapped/detained in Niger in 2025; (4) The March 21 update added health risk indicators, suggesting comprehensive review; (5) The timeline is tight - only ~2 months until July 1, and advisory changes typically follow clear trends rather than individual incidents. The Sahel violence, while severe, appears to be the chronic baseline already factored into the Level 3 designation rather than an escalating crisis warranting Level 4.
